login_credentials.note: Requires ADMIN role user to access /ai/admin/* routes. All admin routes enforce createAdminModuleBeforeLoad('system') guard.

prerequisite_seed_data: 3 AiModel records (claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5) seeded from E5
6 AiPrompt records seeded from E5 (categories: record-creation, query, analysis, briefing, skill, chat)
Multiple AiSkill records grouped by moduleKey seeded from E5/E5b
1 example automation 'Daily AR Aging Summary' seeded from E5c-1 with 2 steps
6 system AiPromptVariable records seeded from E5c-1 (today, currentUser.name, currentUser.role, company.name, company.baseCurrency, company.defaultCurrency)

journeys.name: AI Configuration Dashboard Overview
journeys.description: Verify the AI Configuration Dashboard loads with summary cards (models, agents, skills, automations), token usage chart, automation health section, and quick-nav cards linking to sub-pages.

steps.expected_result: AI Configuration Dashboard loads
steps.visual_check: Dashboard page with 'AI Configuration' heading, four summary cards (Active Models, Active Agents, Active Skills, Automations) in a grid, Concept D purple theme (#f4f2ff background), Plus Jakarta Sans headings

steps.expected_result: Active Models card shows count (at least 3 from seed) with mono font, subtitle showing monthly cost
Active Agents card shows count badge
Active Skills card shows total count and 'across N modules' subtitle
Automations card shows active count (green badge) and paused count (amber badge)
Token Usage (Last 30 Days) section visible with a chart (line or area) showing input/output tokens
steps.visual_check: Chart area visible with axes labels, Concept D card styling (12px radius, shadow), section heading in font-serif

steps.expected_result: Automation Health section visible with status pie/donut chart, failed runs count, upcoming runs list, token spend trend chart
steps.visual_check: Automation Health section with 2x2 stats grid, 'View All Runs' link, charts with purple gradient fills

steps.expected_result: Quick-nav cards for 'Model Registry', 'Prompt Templates', and 'Automations' are visible and clickable
Navigates to /ai/admin/models
Model Registry page loaded
Back to dashboard
Navigates to /ai/admin/automations
Automations list page loaded

journeys.name: Create a New AI Model
journeys.description: Test the full model creation flow: navigate to Model Registry, click Add Model, fill all fields including routing tags and fallback model, save, and verify the new model appears in the list.

steps.expected_result: Model Registry list page loads
steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List showing seeded models (claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5) with columns: name (mono), provider, model ID, max tokens, cost/M, routing tags (purple badges), status, default flag

steps.expected_result: At least 3 seeded models displayed with correct column values and mono font for name/modelId/tokens/cost
Model form page opens in create mode
steps.visual_check: Model form with 'New Model' heading, breadcrumbs 'AI Administration > Model Registry > New Model', Primary and Advanced tabs, Save/Cancel buttons

steps.expected_result: All primary fields populated
Advanced tab content shows routing tags, capabilities, fallback model, config fields
Routing tags show as purple pill badges, JSON validates, fallback model dropdown excludes self
steps.visual_check: Advanced tab with purple 'standard' and 'vision' tag pills, JSON editor with mono font, fallback model dropdown populated

steps.expected_result: Model created successfully
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Model created' visible, navigated to model detail page showing all saved values

steps.expected_result: Model list now shows the new model
steps.visual_check: Model list with 4 models including 'test-gpt-4o' row with 'openai' provider, 'standard' and 'vision' routing tag badges

journeys.name: Edit Model — Default Toggle and Business Rules
journeys.description: Test editing a model, toggling the default flag (which unsets the previous default), and verifying business rule: cannot deactivate a default model.

steps.expected_result: Model list loads
Model edit form opens with pre-populated data
steps.visual_check: Model form showing 'GPT-4o Test Model' heading, all fields populated with saved values from creation

steps.expected_result: Warning text appears: 'Setting as default will unset the current default model'
Model updated, default flag set
steps.visual_check: Success toast visible, model now shows purple 'Default' badge

steps.expected_result: Only test-gpt-4o shows 'Default' badge, previous default model no longer has it
steps.visual_check: Model list with exactly one 'Default' purple badge on 'test-gpt-4o' row

steps.expected_result: Edit form opens
Toggle switched off
422 error: 'Cannot deactivate the default model. Assign a new default first.'
steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error message about not being able to deactivate the default model

journeys.name: Model List Search and Delete Guard
journeys.description: Test model list search functionality and verify deletion guard: a model referenced by agents cannot be deleted.

steps.expected_result: Model list loads with all models
List filters to show only claude-opus-4-6
steps.visual_check: Search input populated with 'opus', list shows only matching model(s)

steps.expected_result: List resets to show all models

steps.target: Overflow menu on claude-opus-4-6 row (model referenced by agents)
steps.expected_result: Dropdown menu with Edit, Toggle Active, Delete options
Confirmation dialog appears
422 error: 'This model is referenced by N agents. Remove agent references first.'
steps.visual_check: Error toast explaining model cannot be deleted because agents reference it

Prompt created successfully with version 1

journeys.name: Prompt Versioning — Edit, Diff, and Restore
journeys.description: Test prompt version management: edit a prompt (requires changeReason), view version history with diff, and restore a previous version.

steps.expected_result: Prompt list loads
Prompt editor page opens with saved data

input_data.systemPrompt: You are a senior accounts receivable specialist for {{company.name}}. Today is {{today}}. Your role is to generate professional but firm reminder emails for overdue invoices. Always include the exact amount owed.

steps.expected_result: System prompt text updated
Change reason modal appears
steps.visual_check: Modal dialog with title 'Save Prompt Changes', textarea for change reason (required), Cancel and Save buttons

input_data.changeReason: Added seniority and instruction to include exact amount owed

steps.expected_result: Change reason entered

steps.target: Save button in change reason modal
steps.expected_result: Version 2 created, version sidebar updated
steps.visual_check: Success toast, version sidebar now shows v2 (active, with purple left border) and v1 below it, change reason visible on v2

steps.target: Version v1 in the version sidebar
steps.expected_result: Diff view shown between v1 and current active version v2
steps.visual_check: Diff view with additions in green background (+) and deletions in red background (-), showing the system prompt changes between v1 and v2

steps.expected_result: 'Restore This Version' button visible for selected old version v1
Version 3 created with v1 content, change reason auto-populated as 'Restored from version 1'
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Version restored. Now active: v3', version sidebar now shows v3 (active) with change reason 'Restored from version 1', v2, and v1

journeys.name: Create a Scheduled Automation with Steps

steps.expected_result: Automation list page loads

steps.expected_result: Automation builder page opens in create mode

input_data.name: Weekly PO Review
input_data.description: Review open purchase orders every Monday morning and flag overdue deliveries

steps.expected_result: Name and description populated

steps.target: Scheduled radio button in trigger type
steps.expected_result: Cron builder component appears with schedule fields and timezone selector

steps.expected_result: Cron fields update to 0 9 * * 1, preview shows 'At 9:00 AM, on Monday'

steps.target: Add Step button in steps section
steps.expected_result: First step card appears with agent dropdown, goal textarea, and I/O config panels

input_data.goal: Review all open purchase orders. Flag any with delivery dates more than 7 days overdue. For each flagged PO, assess risk level.

steps.expected_result: Step 1 configured with agent and goal
Step 2 card appears below step 1 with connector line between them

input_data.goal: Summarise the flagged purchase orders and draft a report for the procurement manager.

steps.expected_result: Step 2 configured

steps.target: 'Use Previous Step Output' shortcut on Step 2
steps.expected_result: Step 2 input config auto-populated with PREVIOUS_STEP source referencing step 1
Budget fields set to 75,000 tokens and 600 seconds
Automation created successfully
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Automation created', navigated to automation edit page showing all saved configuration including 2 steps, schedule, and budget
